simile_bans: no `X like Y` similes where Y is domestic or food
no similes comparing the dead or decaying to objects
no extended metaphors longer than one sentence
no similes whose vehicle is a household item
no metaphors built on weather or seasons
no metaphors comparing human action to animal behavior
no similes whose vehicle is a tool or weapon
no metaphors built on light or darkness
cadence_bans: no staccato comma-list cadences (e.g., `throat, chest, skull`)
no sentences that end on a single-word coda
no parallel-clause balanced sentences (`he did X. she did Y.`)
no consecutive sentences of the same length
no sentences shorter than five words
syntactic_bans: no negative-construction sentences (`he did not X`)
no sentences beginning with a participial phrase
no colons used to introduce clauses
imagery_bans: no glowbug, lantern, or torchlight throwing shadows imagery
no birds rising from trees as a reaction to violence
no character described by what their hands are doing
no description of breath as visible vapor
no eyes described as reflective surfaces (mirrors, water, glass)
no silence described as a physical weight
register_bans: no clinical or scientific vocabulary in narration
no archaic register if the scene is modern
no modern slang if the scene is pre-modern
no abstract nouns standing in for sensory description
no philosophical asides from the narrator
no Latinate vocabulary where Anglo-Saxon would do
